calendar.matters:
Notice Of Ex Parte Application And Ex Parte Application For Temporary Restraining Order And Request For Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Issue Prohibiting Defendants From Selling Real Property Pending Trial
Off Calendar Feb-05-2019 Off Calendar

Petition To Remove Trustee And Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
Off Calendar Sep-02-2016 Denied

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Bond
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman (interim temporary trustee) in Propria Persona. David Simmons in Propria Persona.

Mr. Freedman stated that bond was filed this morning. Mr. Simmons stated that he objects, that this has been a long procedure and that there is fraud going on. Mr. Freedman stated that he is neutral, that the parties can litigate their disputes and that he has a plan to administer the trust. The Court stated that as the bond has been filed the status will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Taylor for Marquez Simmons.

The Court confirmed that David Simmons is not present and stated that we are here on a petition for appointment of a successor trustee. Mr. Taylor stated that they had requested that either Michael Freedman or Debra Dolch be appointed, that they would like the Court to appoint Michael Freedman stating that he practices before this court and has been an attorney for a long time. The Court asked if Mr. Freeman has specific ties or a relationship to this case and was told no. The Court stated that it sees that the petitioner identified both Michael Freedman and Debra Dolch, that notice is appropriate, that it concurs that a successor trustee is necessary and will grant the petition appointing Michael Freedman. The Court further stated that David Simmons is not present to oppose. The Court granted the petition to appoint successor trustee (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Continued To Jul-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Continued To Sep-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Continued To Jun-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar May-24-2016 Off Calendar

Off Calendar May-24-2016 Off Calendar

Petition To Remove Trustee And Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Continued To Sep-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Mark Simmons As Executor, Trustee Of The Simmons Family Revocable Living Trust Estate
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Marquez Simmons As Executor, Trustee Of The Simmons Family Revocable Living Trust Estate
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
Off Calendar Apr-19-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-15-299221 Estate of Josephine Simmons and PTR-16-299464 The Simmons Family Revocable Living Trust.
Appearances: William Taylor for Marquez Simmons. Mila Lvovich for Mark Simmons. David Simmons in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that we are hear on the estate and trust matters, that in the trust is a petition to confirm trust assets, in the estate is a petition to probate, that ultimately the property will end up in the trust, that it is not clear if there is more evidence as to why it was taken out of the trust, that there is an issue of trusteeship, that it believes there should be a neutral trustee, that it is not comfortable with a felon serving as trustee, nor is it attracted to appointing Marquez, that the matter is hotly contested, that Marquez has no claim to the trusteeship, that with a neutral trustee the parties may be able to work something out, that it will continue the matter for a period of time for the parties to agree or nominate a neutral trustee and that with a neutral in place they can figure out where the property belongs. Mr. Taylor stated that there is no argument from Marquez other than what is in the papers. Ms. Lvovich stated that she feels it is a good idea but will need to speak with her client. The Court stated that it will need an amended petition, that it hopes the parties can agree on a neutral and that if they cannot agree it will need their respective petitions regarding their choice. Mr. Simmons stated that he is the next heir. The Court responded that this case does not involve your father's estate, that whatever happened in your father's case has to be litigated in that case. Ms. Lvovich asked what to do with the objection to the administration of the estate. The Court stated that they need to get a trustee in place, that evidence may surface regarding the property and that if it becomes clear that the property is in the trust there may not be a need for probate. Mr. Taylor stated that the will is a pour over will. The Court responded that that does not mean that it can grant an 850 petition to put this into the trust and that if there is no personal representative you cannot notice that person. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (case 299221), the petition for order confirming trust assets and the petition to remove trustee and appoint successor trustee (case 299464) were continued to May 24, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that amendments with property notice will be on file nominating a neutral trustee.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Trustee And Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-19-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Continued To Apr-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
